unwritten_customs: No widow sits alone on Bell Night.
A family debt is not named aloud before harvest unless the speaker wants a feud.
If the midwife asks for silence, even enemies pretend to be discreet.

political_shifts: Ruth Vale has held civic influence long enough for duty to resemble dynasty.
1902: The council reopened the sluice dispute

char_ruth_vale.family_origin: Vale mill money and civic office
char_ruth_vale.private_voice: She counts affection the way other people count rent: carefully, because once it cost too much.
char_ruth_vale.public_voice: Measured, civic, and faintly maternal even when she is closing a door.
char_ruth_vale.poetic_voice: A bell of office rung from beneath old water.

memories.summary: Ruth Vale died in autumn when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.

secrets.summary: She buried the proof that Silas profited from relief money.
secrets.truth: I kept the flood ledger because it showed Silas drawing relief funds into the mill while the Harrows pawned blankets.

reputation.public_summary: Ruth finally said aloud what the town had only guessed.

char_silas_vale.public_voice: A tired patriarch who still expects the room to arrange itself around him.
char_silas_vale.poetic_voice: A mill wheel speaking after it has broken a hand too many.

secrets.summary: He treated flood aid as private capital.
secrets.truth: I drew relief money into the mill and let gratitude stand in for wages because power was easier to keep than fairness.

reputation.public_summary: Silas finally said aloud what the town had only guessed.

char_caleb_vale.family_origin: Vale mill money and civic office
char_caleb_vale.private_voice: He wishes for a life that sounds less inherited.
char_caleb_vale.public_voice: Polite, learned, and almost too careful to be trusted.
char_caleb_vale.poetic_voice: A classroom window left open toward the cemetery.

secrets.summary: He keeps Miriam's letters under a geography atlas.
secrets.truth: I kept Miriam Harrow's letters under the school atlas and called that arrangement restraint when it was only cowardice with stationery.

reputation.public_summary: Caleb is praised for gentleness in a town that says the word as if it were a warning.

secrets.summary: He once altered the sluice markers to embarrass the Vales.

secrets.summary: She altered Mercy Bell's birth record.
secrets.truth: I changed Mercy Bell's father in the birth ledger because the living men would have survived the gossip and the dead woman would not.

secrets.summary: She would leave town with Caleb if he chose courage.
secrets.truth: I said I wanted nothing from Caleb Vale that the town could name, and I would have boarded the first train with him if he had arrived at the station before dawn.

reputation.public_summary: Miriam Harrow is liked better in private than in committee, which she considers a compliment.

secrets.summary: She keeps Gideon Bell's old love letters hidden behind the bar.
secrets.truth: I kept Gideon Bell's letters to the singer from Galena behind the brandy shelf because holiness deserves at least one witness who can laugh.

reputation.public_summary: Ada looked composed while hiding a strain no one measured correctly.

char_thomas_quill.family_origin: Quill rooms above the Blue Lantern
char_thomas_quill.private_voice: Death made him patient with bodies and impatient with vanity.
char_thomas_quill.public_voice: Reserved, careful, and unexpectedly courtly when grief enters the room.
char_thomas_quill.poetic_voice: Black wool, cedar shavings, and the soft authority of a man who closes lids.

He watched Mercy Bell wash a dead child with such steadiness that desire began in him as reverence.

secrets.summary: He has built a room he hopes Mercy Bell will one day use.
secrets.truth: I built a spare room over the workshop and measured it for Mercy Bell before I had even held her hand properly.

reputation.public_summary: Thomas Quill is the kind of man people call steady when they do not know how lonely they mean.

char_clara_quill.private_voice: She hears escape in any tune played softly enough.
char_clara_quill.public_voice: Half dutiful, half already leaving in imagination.

char_gideon_bell.public_voice: Sonorous, composed, and offended by appetite in whatever body it occupies.
char_gideon_bell.poetic_voice: A cracked hymn book telling the truth between verses.

He nearly ran away with a singer from Galena and spent the next twenty years preaching against the temptation of itinerant charm.

secrets.summary: He wrote love letters he still calls a spiritual lapse.
secrets.truth: I wrote letters to a singer and nearly left the parish with her, then preached twice as hard because desire had learned my handwriting.

reputation.public_summary: Gideon looked composed while hiding a strain no one measured correctly.

char_gideon_bell.last_reflection: Keep a dangerous truth tucked under ordinary gestures.

char_mercy_bell.private_voice: She confuses usefulness with deserving love and knows the confusion may ruin her.
char_mercy_bell.public_voice: Calm in crisis, distant when anyone asks how she is sleeping.
char_mercy_bell.poetic_voice: Bandage, winter lamp, and a pulse counted after everyone else stops listening.

memories.summary: Mercy Bell died in autumn when work, worry, and weather finally agreed against the body.
Mercy Bell spoke to Eliza Harrow without armor and made a little peace where habit had expected theatre.
Mercy Bell spent the season in reflection, revising private beliefs against the evidence of time.
Mercy Bell worked at being necessary, which is the nearest thing some lives come to applause.
Thomas Quill held a basin for her without making a performance of pity, and she has trusted the gesture longer than she trusts language.

secrets.summary: She suspects Silas Vale is her father.
secrets.truth: I have long suspected that Silas Vale, not any Bell, gave me my face, and I hated the suspicion because it felt too much like belonging to the wrong room.

reputation.public_summary: Mercy Bell is the person most people want nearby in a crisis and least understand afterward.

char_b7a8da5c6f.family_origin: Working mill folk who remember every insult as labor already paid for.
char_b7a8da5c6f.private_voice: Raised between Caleb's restraint and Miriam's appetite for feeling.
char_b7a8da5c6f.public_voice: Too young to choose a public performance.
char_b7a8da5c6f.poetic_voice: A future voice still taking shape in the family weather.
char_b7a8da5c6f.biography: Born in 1901 to Caleb Vale and Miriam Harrow.

town_events.title: The Thaw Market reopened credit and appetite
town_events.description: Merchants extended trust on thin margins, and old arguments returned with the river road.
town_events.economic_shift: spring credit loosened for one hopeful month

town_events.title: Lantern Regatta made the river look forgiving
town_events.description: For one night the river carried lanterns, flirtation, and the illusion that grudges could drift away without naming themselves.

town_events.title: Harvest Supper staged every alliance in public
town_events.description: At the long tables, compliments sounded like contracts and silence carried more weight than the bread.

town_events.title: Bell Night made widows and liars sing together
town_events.description: During Bell Night the cemetery lamps were lit, the names of the dead were read aloud, and living people behaved like witnesses to one another.

town_events.title: The council reopened the sluice dispute
town_events.description: At town hall the old quarrel over the mill sluice was argued again, with the Harrows and Vales listening as if the river kept score.
town_events.economic_shift: the mill contract wavered under civic pressure

town_events.title: A heat spell pushed the clinic past mercy
town_events.description: The heat laid children low, drew old complaints out of the bones, and made the clinic smell of vinegar and impatience.

town_events.title: Harvest Supper staged every alliance in public
town_events.description: At the long tables, compliments sounded like contracts and silence carried more weight than the bread.

town_events.title: Bell Night made widows and liars sing together
town_events.description: During Bell Night the cemetery lamps were lit, the names of the dead were read aloud, and living people behaved like witnesses to one another.

chronicles.title: Morrowfield 1901: Contracts and Lanterns
chronicles.summary: Mercy Bell was carried into town memory as autumn pressed work, worry, and weather against the body, while Caleb Vale and Miriam Harrow turned careful courtship into a civic marriage under public scrutiny. Arthur Harrow arrived carrying several families at once, and every old loyalty in Morrowfield bent around the cradle, even as Silas Vale renewed a family bitterness against Jonah Harrow. The church staged Harvest Supper where compliments sounded like contracts, the town hall reopened credit and appetite at the Thaw Market, Bell Night made widows and liars sing together at the cemetery, and the Lantern Regatta let the river look forgiving for a night.
contradictions: Gideon Bell kept the face the town expected
Thomas Quill listened to an inward verdict

chronicles.title: : Autumn's Sharp Echoes in Morrowfield
chronicles.summary: Ruth Vale died in autumn as work, worry, and weather finally overcame her. Throughout the year, Ruth and Jonah Harrow traded sharp rumors and renewed old grievances, while Silas Vale spread a tale of Jonah's affairs that reached the market by noon. The town council revisited the sluice dispute at town hall, a heat spell overwhelmed the clinic with the smell of vinegar and impatience, and the Harvest Supper at the church turned compliments into contracts and silence into weight.
contradictions: Gideon Bell kept the face the town expected
Ada Quill kept the face the town expected

epitaphs.voice: Bandage, winter lamp, and a pulse counted after everyone else stops listening.
epitaphs.text: I kept the winter lamp lit while bandage met skin and pulse answered after the room went quiet; usefulness bloomed like frost, and I called it love. Thomas Quill held the basin without spectacle, and I kept that hush longer than any vow. Gideon Bell kept the face the town expected, and I carried Mercy Bell into town memory—autumn at last agreed with the body, and I, who feared dependency and gossip, let care count me beyond myself, close enough to be wanted, far enough to remain a stranger to my own sleep. I confused deserving with being needed, and the confusion, sweet and ruinous, outlived me like a small, steady pulse in the cold.
hidden_truths: I have long suspected that Silas Vale, not any Bell, gave me my face, and I hated the suspicion because it felt too much like belonging to the wrong room.
public_contradictions: Mercy Bell is the person most people want nearby in a crisis and least understand afterward.

epitaphs.voice: A bell of office rung from beneath old water.
epitaphs.text: : I, Ruth Vale, stood as a bell of office rung from beneath old water, counting affection like rent because once it cost too much. In autumn, work, worry, and weather finally agreed against my body, and I spoke aloud what the town had only guessed.
hidden_truths: I kept the flood ledger that revealed Silas drawing relief funds into the mill while the Harrows pawned blankets.
Silas Vale told me a truth so long concealed it changed us both before supper.
public_contradictions: Her public voice was measured, civic, and faintly maternal, yet her private voice counted affection like rent, revealing pride and coldness.
She desired legacy and control, yet feared disgrace and obsolescence, a tension between her civic stature and inner fear.
